data.title: Small-Cap communication services stocks ranked by quant ratings after earnings season
data.description:
As the earnings season winds down, investors are turning their attention to updated quant ratings following the latest round of corporate results. The scores provide a snapshot of how companies rank across key factors such as valuation, growth, profitability, momentum, and revisions after reporting their quarterly performance.

Below is a snapshot of small-cap communication services companies with market capitalizations between $30M and $2B, highlighting those with the highest and lowest quant ratings after the earnings season, underscoring the stocks that strengthened their fundamentals as well as those that lagged.

data.title: Insider trades: Bank of America, Visa among notable names this week
data.description:
Bank of America (BAC), Costco (COST), and Expand Energy (EXE) were among the companies that reported insider transactions this week. The following is a list of deals that occurred between March 9 and March 13.

  • Bank of America’s (BAC) President, International Bernard Mensah, sold 94,000 shares at $46.94 per share for a total value of $4.41M, leaving him with 170,184 shares.
  • Visa (V) Director Lloyd Carney disposed of 650 shares at $309.62 per share, generating $201,253. Carney retains 2,679 shares following the sale.
  • At Altria (MO), Chief HR Officer and CCO Charles Whitaker sold 27,908 shares at $67.57 per share for $1.89M, bringing his holdings down to 181,886 shares.
  • Costco’s (COST) Executive Vice President Claudine Adamo sold 730 shares at $1,003.02 per share in a transaction valued at $732,205, leaving her with 6,121 shares.
  • ConocoPhillips (COP) Director Timothy Leach sold 40,000 shares at $118.79 per share for proceeds of $4.75M, reducing his stake to 411,211 shares.
  • American Tower (AMT) Director Rajesh Kalathur purchased 2,671 shares at $185.30 per share in a transaction valued at $494,936, bringing his total holdings to 7,372 shares.
  • Expand Energy’s (EXE) interim president and CEO, Michael Wichterich, acquired 2,000 shares at $107.50 per share for a total worth of $215,000, increasing his stake to 83,498 shares.
  • C3.ai (AI) director Jim Snabe bought 25,000 shares at $9 per share, amounting to $225,000. The transaction has lifted his total holdings to 423,000.
  • Coupang (CPNG) director Neil Mehta purchased a stake worth $136.56M. He bought a total of 7.35M shares in the price range of $18.4 to $18.64 in three separate transactions. Mehta owns a total of 55.39M shares following the purchase.

data.title: SA Asks: What's next for Qualcomm as it faces increased competition?
data.description:
What's next for Qualcomm (QCOM) as it faces increased competition in the smartphone chip market?

Seeking Alpha analysts APAC Investment News and Bay Area Ideas weigh in. 

APAC Investment News: The management team clearly felt the competition in the smartphone space closing in, and they’ve already been pivoting into automotive, edge AI, and other pioneering opportunities. There’s no guarantee these moves will work out, of course, but the smartphone space, from Qualcomm's perspective, is dying. Apple (AAPL) continues to gain market share vs. Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Android, Samsung (SSNLF) is shifting in-house, and Chinese companies are going domestic. It's pivot or suffer a slow death, in my opinion, but Qualcomm's leadership’s proactive approach has me feeling confident.

Bay Area Ideas: Qualcomm (QCOM) is indeed facing stiff competition on multiple fronts. In the smartphone space, they have to deal with in-house chips and new competitors in the Chinese market. In areas like automotive and AI, they have to compete with the likes of Nvidia (NVDA). The stock is clearly pricing in these competitive pressures, but I believe the market may be overlooking that opportunities have also increased significantly in recent years.

Looking forward, despite some memory headwinds, there's a looming AI PC upgrade cycle that wouldn't be happening if it wasn't for the AI revolution. Snapdragon X2 Plus is a competitive product that's well positioned for this wave. Another opportunity that I highlighted in my recent article was robotics. At this point, physical AI is one of the leading frontiers that holds the most promise. Dragonwing IQ10 is a state-of-the-art chip, and their recent collaboration with NEURA Robotics shows that adoption is starting off well. Lastly, there are opportunities like their role in 6G.

To conclude, I would say that while they're seeing as much competition as ever, they're also seeing opportunities that didn't exist just a few years ago. In the areas discussed above, the opportunities are large enough for multiple winners, so Qualcomm's growth still has a solid chance of re-accelerating over the long run.

  • Top Semiconductor Stocks

data.title: Mid-Cap healthcare stocks ranked by quant ratings after earnings season
data.description:
As earnings season winds down, investors are shifting focus to updated quant ratings following the latest wave of corporate results. The scores offer a snapshot of how companies rank across key factors such as valuation, growth, profitability, momentum, and estimate revisions after reporting quarterly performance.

Of note, the State Street Health Care Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLV) which accounts for a substantial 12.12% of the S&P 500, has emerged as a laggard in the current market environment. The XLV has retreated 3.00% year-to-date, outpacing the 2.53% slide seen in the benchmark index.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant system rates stocks on a scale from 1 to 5, classifying ratings of 3.5 or above as bullish and 2.5 or below as bearish.

Post-earnings quant scorecard: Below is a summary of mid-cap real estate companies with market capitalizations between $2B and $10B ranked by their current factor scores.

Top-quant rated stocks:

BrightSpring Health Services (BTSG), Quant Rating: 4.83, Strong Buy.   

PACS Group (PACS), Quant Rating: 4.78, Strong Buy.

Indivior Pharmaceuticals (INDV), Quant Rating: 4.76, Strong Buy.           

Liquidia Corporation (LQDA), Quant Rating: 4.46, Buy.     

Guardian Pharmacy Services (GRDN), Quant Rating: 4.29, Buy.

Bottom quant rated stocks:

Corcept Therapeutics (CORT), Quant Rating: 1.04, Strong Sell. 

Doximity (DOCS), Quant Rating: 1.16, Strong Sell.

Telix Pharmaceuticals Limited (TLX), Quant Rating: 1.18, Strong Sell.

Stevanato Group (STVN), Quant Rating: 1.20, Strong Sell.             

Hims & Hers Health (HIMS), Quant Rating: 1.26, Strong Sell.

data.title: Highest and lowest quant-rated healthcare stocks above $10B cap after earnings season
Elon Musk says Tesla Terafab project launching in a week
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk announced on Saturday that a project that enables in-house production of semiconductors for Tesla (TSLA) will be launched in a week.

In an X post, the billionaire investor wrote that the project named Terafab "launches in 7 days,” signaling the beginning of a massive undertaking that could cost billions of dollars but could ease supply constraints for the company and help it face potential geopolitical risks.

In January, Musk suggested that Tesla's (TSLA) existing chip suppliers, Samsung Electronics (SSNLF), Micron Technology (MU), and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), cannot supply the EV maker at the levels it needs.

“In order to remove the probable constraint in three or four years, we are going to have to build a Tesla TeraFab." Musk said during the company's earnings call, adding, “A very big fab that includes logic, memory, and packaging, domestically.”

“That’s going to be very important to ensure we are protected against geopolitical risks,” he said. “I think people maybe are underweighting some of the geopolitical risks that are going to be a major factor in a few years.”
